ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: A supplicant asked for a punishment bound to happen
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: A challenger has demanded a punishment bound to come

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: To the disbelievers; of it there is no preventer.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: for the disbelievers—to be averted by none—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [It is] from Allah, owner of the ways of ascent.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: from Allah, Lord of pathways of ˹heavenly˺ ascent,

ayahs.textArabic: تَعۡرُجُ ٱلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةُ وَٱلرُّوحُ إِلَيۡهِ فِي يَوۡمٖ كَانَ مِقۡدَارُهُۥ خَمۡسِينَ أَلۡفَ سَنَةٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: TaAAruju almalaikatu waalrroohu ilayhi fee yawmin kana miqdaruhu khamseena alfa sanatin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The angels and the Spirit will ascend to Him during a Day the extent of which is fifty thousand years.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹through which˺ the angels and the ˹holy˺ spirit will ascend to Him on a Day fifty thousand years in length.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So be patient with gracious patience.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So endure ˹this denial, O Prophet,˺ with beautiful patience.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, they see it [as] distant,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They truly see this ˹Day˺ as impossible,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But We see it [as] near.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but We see it as inevitable.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: On the Day the sky will be like murky oil,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that Day the sky will be like molten brass

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the mountains will be like wool,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the mountains like ˹tufts of˺ wool.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And no friend will ask [anything of] a friend,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And no close friend will ask ˹about˺ their friends,

ayahs.textArabic: يُبَصَّرُونَهُمۡۚ يَوَدُّ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمُ لَوۡ يَفۡتَدِي مِنۡ عَذَابِ يَوۡمِئِذِۭ بِبَنِيهِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yubassaroonahum yawaddu almujrimu law yaftadee min AAathabi yawmiithin bibaneehi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will be shown each other. The criminal will wish that he could be ransomed from the punishment of that Day by his children
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹although˺ they will be made to see each other. The wicked will wish to ransom themselves from the punishment of that Day by their children,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And his wife and his brother
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: their spouses, their siblings,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And his nearest kindred who shelter him
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: their clan that sheltered them,

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَن فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ جَمِيعٗا ثُمَّ يُنجِيهِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waman fee alardi jameeAAan thumma yunjeehi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And whoever is on earth entirely [so] then it could save him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and everyone on earth altogether, just to save themselves.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! Indeed, it is the Flame [of Hell],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! There will certainly be a raging Flame

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: A remover of exteriors.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ripping off scalps.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It invites he who turned his back [on truth] and went away [from obedience]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It will summon whoever turned their backs ˹on Allah˺ and turned away ˹from the truth˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And collected [wealth] and hoarded.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and gathered and hoarded ˹wealth˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, mankind was created anxious:
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, humankind was created impatient:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When evil touches him, impatient,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: distressed when touched with evil,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when good touches him, withholding [of it],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and withholding when touched with good—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except the observers of prayer -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except those who pray,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Those who are constant in their prayer
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: consistently performing their prayers;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those within whose wealth is a known right
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and who give the rightful share of their wealth

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: For the petitioner and the deprived -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: to the beggar and the poor;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who believe in the Day of Recompense
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and who ˹firmly˺ believe in the Day of Judgment;

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ هُم مِّنۡ عَذَابِ رَبِّهِم مُّشۡفِقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena hum min AAathabi rabbihim mushfiqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who are fearful of the punishment of their Lord -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and those who fear the punishment of their Lord—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the punishment of their Lord is not that from which one is safe -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹knowing that˺ none should feel secure from their Lord’s punishment—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who guard their private parts
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and those who guard their chastity

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا عَلَىٰٓ أَزۡوَٰجِهِمۡ أَوۡ مَا مَلَكَتۡ أَيۡمَٰنُهُمۡ فَإِنَّهُمۡ غَيۡرُ مَلُومِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa AAala azwajihim aw ma malakat aymanuhum fainnahum ghayru maloomeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except from their wives or those their right hands possess, for indeed, they are not to be blamed -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except with their wives or those ˹bondwomen˺ in their possession, for then they are free from blame,

ayahs.textArabic: فَمَنِ ٱبۡتَغَىٰ وَرَآءَ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُوْلَٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلۡعَادُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Famani ibtagha waraa thalika faolaika humu alAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But whoever seeks beyond that, then they are the transgressors -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but whoever seeks beyond that are the transgressors.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who are to their trusts and promises attentive
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹The faithful are˺ also those who are true to their trusts and covenants;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who are in their testimonies upright
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and who are honest in their testimony;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who [carefully] maintain their prayer:
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and who are ˹properly˺ observant of their prayers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will be in gardens, honored.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: These will be in Gardens, held in honour.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So what is [the matter] with those who disbelieve, hastening [from] before you, [O Muhammad],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So what is the matter with the disbelievers that they rush ˹head-long˺ towards you ˹O Prophet˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [To sit] on [your] right and [your] left in separate groups?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: from the right and the left, in groups ˹to mock you˺?

ayahs.textArabic: أَيَطۡمَعُ كُلُّ ٱمۡرِيٕٖ مِّنۡهُمۡ أَن يُدۡخَلَ جَنَّةَ نَعِيمٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: AyatmaAAu kullu imriin minhum an yudkhala jannata naAAeemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Does every person among them aspire to enter a garden of pleasure?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Does every one of them expect to be admitted into a Garden of Bliss?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! Indeed, We have created them from that which they know.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! Indeed, they ˹already˺ know what We created them from.

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَآ أُقۡسِمُ بِرَبِّ ٱلۡمَشَٰرِقِ وَٱلۡمَغَٰرِبِ إِنَّا لَقَٰدِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fala oqsimu birabbi almashariqi waalmagharibi inna laqadiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So I swear by the Lord of [all] risings and settings that indeed We are able
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So, I do swear by the Lord of ˹all˺ the points of sunrise and sunset that We are truly capable

ayahs.textArabic: عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّبَدِّلَ خَيۡرٗا مِّنۡهُمۡ وَمَا نَحۡنُ بِمَسۡبُوقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: AAala an nubaddila khayran minhum wama nahnu bimasbooqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: To replace them with better than them; and We are not to be outdone.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: of replacing them with ˹others˺ better than them, and We cannot be prevented ˹from doing so˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فَذَرۡهُمۡ يَخُوضُواْ وَيَلۡعَبُواْ حَتَّىٰ يُلَٰقُواْ يَوۡمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatharhum yakhoodoo wayalAAaboo hatta yulaqoo yawmahumu allathee yooAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So let them indulge ˹in falsehood˺ and amuse ˹themselves˺ until they face their Day, which they have been threatened with—

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ يَخۡرُجُونَ مِنَ ٱلۡأَجۡدَاثِ سِرَاعٗا كَأَنَّهُمۡ إِلَىٰ نُصُبٖ يُوفِضُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma yakhrujoona mina alajdathi siraAAan kaannahum ila nusubin yoofidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day they will emerge from the graves rapidly as if they were, toward an erected idol, hastening.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Day they will come forth from the graves swiftly, as if racing to an idol ˹for a blessing˺,

ayahs.textArabic: خَٰشِعَةً أَبۡصَٰرُهُمۡ تَرۡهَقُهُمۡ ذِلَّةٞۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلۡيَوۡمُ ٱلَّذِي كَانُواْ يُوعَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: KhashiAAatan absaruhum tarhaquhum thillatun thalika alyawmu allathee kanoo yooAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: with eyes downcast, utterly covered with disgrace. That is the Day they have ˹always˺ been warned of.
